onboarding.welcomeSub: Private messaging that's yours. No phone number, no email — nothing a company can read or take away.
onboarding.pickSub: Choose your account to open it, or add another.
onboarding.forgetConfirm: Remove {{name}} from this device? Their messages here will be erased. You can sign back in anytime with the 12 words.
onboarding.nameSub: This is the name your contacts will see. You can change it anytime.
onboarding.kitSub: These 12 words are your account. Use them to sign in on another device — your phone, a laptop — or to get back in if you ever lose this one.
onboarding.kitWarn: Keep them private: anyone with these words can use your account, and Vox cannot recover them for you.
onboarding.lockSub: Set a 6-digit PIN to open the app on this device — like your phone's passcode. It's for this device only, and separate from your 12 words.
onboarding.lockAgain: Enter your PIN again to confirm.
onboarding.pinMismatch: Those didn't match — let's try again.
onboarding.restoreSub: Enter your 12 words to sign in on this device. On your other device you'll find them under "Use on another device".
onboarding.unlockSub: Enter your PIN to open Vox on this device.
onboarding.wrongPin: Wrong PIN. Try again, or use your 12 words.
onboarding.forgotPin: Forgot your PIN? Add the account again with your 12 words
onboarding.unlockBiometric: Unlock with Touch ID / Windows Hello
onboarding.biometricFailed: Couldn't verify. Enter your PIN instead.
onboarding.biometricEnabled: Biometric unlock is on for this device.
onboarding.biometricSetupFailed: Couldn't set up biometric unlock on this device. Your PIN still works.

chat.shareLink: Share this link so someone can add you:
chat.tapInviteShare: Tap New chat to add someone.
chat.selectContact: Select a contact, or share your invite link to start a chat.
chat.e2eNote: Messages are end-to-end encrypted and stored only on your devices.
chat.newGroupChatSub: A private group chat + call for your team or family
chat.addExplainTitle: Vox has no usernames or phone numbers.
chat.addExplainBody: You add someone by sharing a private link or QR code — like giving a friend your number, but no server ever sees who you talk to.
chat.yourInviteHint: Show this code in person, or send the link. Anyone who has it can add you.
chat.yourInviteSide: Let someone scan this with their Vox camera, or copy the link below.
chat.addByLinkHint: Got a Vox invite from someone? Paste it here.
chat.newGroupCallSub: Start a group call with family or friends
chat.invalidInvite: That doesn't look like a valid Vox invite link.

meeting.e2eNote: End-to-end encrypted. Anyone with the link can join — no account needed.
meeting.newMeetingSub: Start a call and share a link — guests join with no account

directory.findHint: Search by name or @handle on this relay.
directory.beFindableHint: Claim an @handle so others on this relay can add you by name.

prompts.relayPrompt: Relay server — e.g. wss://chat.myfamily.com (leave blank to use this site):
prompts.signOutConfirm: Sign out removes this account and its history from this device. You can only restore it with your 12-word phrase. Continue?

admin.title: 🛠️ Relay admin
admin.wrongToken: Wrong admin token (or admin is disabled on this relay).
admin.loginSub: Enter this relay's admin token to manage members and federated peers.
admin.giveToMember: Give this to a new member — they redeem it in the app via "Join private network".
admin.activityNote: Every admin action, with the token fingerprint and IP behind it.

network.title: Host the network
network.intro: This desktop can help run Vox. There are no company servers — desktops like yours are the network.
network.desktopOnly: Hosting runs in the Vox desktop app. Web and mobile are clients.
network.footNote: However you help, messages stay end-to-end encrypted — a host only ever sees sealed ciphertext, never your content. The more desktops host, the bigger the meetings the network can carry.

host.title: Help host the network
host.desc: Run a post office on this device: deliver messages when your contacts are offline, and forward encrypted traffic for big meetings when your connection can spare it. Vox may offer you a meeting to carry when you're a strong, reachable host — always your choice, never automatic.
host.statusOn: Hosting on port {{port}} — you're helping run the network.
host.statusOff: Off — you're a plain client on someone else's host.
host.note: You only ever see ciphertext — never message content, whether it's your circle's mail or a meeting you help carry.
